data.summary_suggestion: Grant permission to subject for resource
data.description_suggestion: Grants a permission to a subject (user or app) for a specific resource. Requires authentication. Accepts permission details in the request body including the resource ID, permission type, and access level. Returns the created permission object. This operation modifies access control rules and takes effect immediately.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are identical and extremely vague. They lack actionable details about what permission is being granted, to whom, for what resource, and what the operation returns. Added context about authentication requirements, expected inputs (resource ID, permission type, access level), return value, and side effects (immediate access control changes) to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.summary_suggestion: Revoke permission from subject for resource
data.description_suggestion: Revokes a previously granted permission from a subject (user or app) for a specific resource. Requires authentication. This operation immediately removes access control rules and takes effect instantly.

Added details about configuration scope (features, auth providers, API settings), return type, and typical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of when this operation should be called.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the Para application's setup and configuration information. This includes enabled features, authentication providers, API settings, and other deployment-specific configuration. Returns a setup configuration object. Typically used during application initialization or to verify deployment settings.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks specificity about what 'setup information' includes. Added details about configuration scope (features, auth providers, API settings), return type, and typical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of when this operation should be called.

message: Operation 'callUtilityGet' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description provides no context about what utility methods are or how to use them. Added explanation of utility method purpose, parameter passing mechanism, return behavior, and deployment-dependent availability to help AI agents understand when and how to invoke this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Execute a utility method via GET request
data.description_suggestion: Invokes a utility method on the Para server using HTTP GET. The method name is specified in the path parameter. Utility methods provide helper functions such as data transformation, validation, or system operations. Query parameters can be passed to the method. Returns the method's result as a JSON response. The specific methods available depend on the Para deployment configuration.
data.reasoning: Original description provides no context about what utility methods are or how to use them. Added explanation of utility method purpose, parameter passing mechanism, return behavior, and deployment-dependent availability to help AI agents understand when and how to invoke this operation.

message: Operation 'getConstraints' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks detail about what constraints are and their purpose. Added explanation of constraint types (required fields, data types, length limits), return structure, and use case to improve AI agent's ability to determine when this operation is needed.

data.current_summary: Get validation constraints for a type
data.current_description: Get validation constraints for a type
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the validation constraints defined for a specific Para object type. Constraints define rules for field validation such as required fields, data types, length limits, and custom validation rules. Returns a constraints object containing all validation rules applicable to the specified type. Used to understand what validation will be applied when creating or updating objects of this type.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks detail about what constraints are and their purpose. Added explanation of constraint types (required fields, data types, length limits), return structure, and use case to improve AI agent's ability to determine when this operation is needed.

message: Operation 'addConstraint' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate. Description lacks specificity about input requirements (constraint definition in body), side effects (affects all future operations), and return value. Added details about constraint persistence, scope of impact, and return type to help AI agents understand the operation's consequences.

data.description_suggestion: Adds or updates a validation constraint for a specific Para object type. The constraint definition is provided in the request body and specifies validation rules such as field requirements, data types, or custom validation logic. The constraint is persisted to the database and will be applied to all subsequent create/update operations for objects of this type. Returns the created or updated constraint object. This operation affects validation behavior for the entire type.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate. Description lacks specificity about input requirements (constraint definition in body), side effects (affects all future operations), and return value. Added details about constraint persistence, scope of impact, and return type to help AI agents understand the operation's consequences.

message: Operation 'getPermissions' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary is clear. Description lacks context about what constitutes a 'subject', what permissions include, and the return structure. Added clarification that subjects can be users/roles/applications, specified permission types (read, write, delete, custom), and use cases to improve AI agent's understanding of permission scope and return format.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all permissions granted to a specific subject (user, role, or application). The subject is identified by its ID in the path parameter. Returns a permissions object containing the list of resources and actions the subject is authorized to access. Permissions can include read, write, delete, and custom actions on various Para object types. Used to verify access rights or audit subject authorization.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear. Description lacks context about what constitutes a 'subject', what permissions include, and the return structure. Added clarification that subjects can be users/roles/applications, specified permission types (read, write, delete, custom), and use cases to improve AI agent's understanding of permission scope and return format.

Operation 'grantPermission' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary and description are identical and extremely vague. They lack actionable details about what permission is being granted, to whom, for what resource, and what the operation returns. Added context about authentication requirements, expected inputs (resource ID, permission type, access level), return value, and side effects (immediate access control changes) to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.summary_suggestion: Grant permission to subject for resource
data.description_suggestion: Grants a permission to a subject (user or app) for a specific resource. Requires authentication. Accepts permission details in the request body including the resource ID, permission type, and access level. Returns the created permission object. This operation modifies access control rules and takes effect immediately.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are identical and extremely vague. They lack actionable details about what permission is being granted, to whom, for what resource, and what the operation returns. Added context about authentication requirements, expected inputs (resource ID, permission type, access level), return value, and side effects (immediate access control changes) to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.summary_suggestion: Revoke permission from subject for resource
data.description_suggestion: Revokes a previously granted permission from a subject (user or app) for a specific resource. Requires authentication. Uses path parameter subjectid and query parameters to specify which permission to revoke. Returns a success response confirming the permission has been removed. This operation immediately removes access control rules and takes effect instantly.

Enhanced version clarifies that both type and ID parameters are required, specifies the response schema (ParaObject), and documents the 404 error case. This improves AI agent understanding of required inputs and possible outcomes.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve a specific object by type and ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a specific object by its type and unique ID. Requires both the object type and ID as path parameters. Returns the complete ParaObject with all properties. Returns 404 if the object does not exist.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are repetitive and lack detail. Enhanced version clarifies that both type and ID parameters are required, specifies the response schema (ParaObject), and documents the 404 error case. This improves AI agent understanding of required inputs and possible outcomes.

message: Operation 'updateObject' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary and description are too generic. Enhanced version clarifies this is a full replacement operation (PUT semantics), specifies required parameters and request schema, explains that all fields must be provided, and documents authentication requirement and error cases. This distinguishes it from PATCH and helps AI agents choose the correct operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Replace an entire object by type and ID
data.description_suggestion: Replace an entire object of the specified type and ID with new data. Accepts a complete ParaObject in the request body. All fields must be provided (full replacement, not partial). Returns the updated ParaObject. Requires authentication. Returns 404 if the object does not exist.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too generic. Enhanced version clarifies this is a full replacement operation (PUT semantics), specifies required parameters and request schema, explains that all fields must be provided, and documents authentication requirement and error cases. This distinguishes it from PATCH and helps AI agents choose the correct operation.

message: Operation 'patchObject' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is adequate but description lacks critical details. Enhanced version clarifies that only provided fields are modified, explains that the response includes all fields, specifies authentication requirement, and documents the 404 error case. This helps AI agents understand the difference between PATCH and PUT operations.

data.description_suggestion: Partially update an object of the specified type and ID. Accepts a partial ParaObject in the request body containing only the fields to be modified. Unspecified fields retain their existing values. Returns the updated ParaObject with all fields. Requires authentication. Returns 404 if the object does not exist.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description lacks critical details. Enhanced version clarifies that only provided fields are modified, explains that the response includes all fields, specifies authentication requirement, and documents the 404 error case. This helps AI agents understand the difference between PATCH and PUT operations.

message: Operation 'deleteObject' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too minimal. Enhanced version clarifies required path parameters, specifies authentication requirement, explains the side effect (permanent deletion), and documents both success (204) and error (404) response codes. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of this operation and its prerequisites.

data.description_suggestion: Delete an object of the specified type and ID. Requires both the object type and ID as path parameters. Requires authentication. Permanently removes the object from the backend. Returns 204 No Content on success or 404 if the object does not exist.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. Enhanced version clarifies required path parameters, specifies authentication requirement, explains the side effect (permanent deletion), and documents both success (204) and error (404) response codes. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of this operation and its prerequisites.

message: Operation 'unlinkObjects' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Original description is extremely vague with no actionable details. Added clarity about what 'unlink' means (removes relationship), specified required parameters (source object type/ID, target object type), explained the side effect (link deletion), and noted the irreversible nature for AI agent decision-making.

data.description_suggestion: Removes an existing link relationship between two Para objects. Requires the type and ID of the source object, and the type of the target object to unlink. The link is deleted from the database. Returns a success response. This operation modifies the link state and cannot be undone without re-creating the link.
data.reasoning: Original description is extremely vague with no actionable details. Added clarity about what 'unlink' means (removes relationship), specified required parameters (source object type/ID, target object type), explained the side effect (link deletion), and noted the irreversible nature for AI agent decision-making.

data.summary_suggestion: Execute a utility method via GET request
data.description_suggestion: Invokes a utility method on the Para server using HTTP GET. The method name is specified in the path parameter. Utility methods provide helper functions such as data transformation, validation, or system operations. Query parameters can be passed to the method. Returns the method's result as a JSON response. The specific methods available depend on the Para deployment configuration.

message: Operation 'getConstraints' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks detail about what constraints are and their purpose. Added explanation of constraint types (required fields, data types, length limits), return structure, and use case to improve AI agent's ability to determine when this operation is needed.

data.current_summary: Get validation constraints for a type
data.current_description: Get validation constraints for a type
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the validation constraints defined for a specific Para object type. Constraints define rules for field validation such as required fields, data types, length limits, and custom validation rules. Returns a constraints object containing all validation rules applicable to the specified type. Used to understand what validation will be applied when creating or updating objects of this type.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks detail about what constraints are and their purpose. Added explanation of constraint types (required fields, data types, length limits), return structure, and use case to improve AI agent's ability to determine when this operation is needed.

message: Operation 'addConstraint' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate. Description lacks critical details about request body format, persistence, scope of effect, and return value. Added information about constraint definition structure, database persistence, application scope, and side effects to help AI agents understand the operation's impact.

data.description_suggestion: Adds or updates a validation constraint for a specific Para object type. The constraint definition is provided in the request body and specifies validation rules such as field requirements, data types, or custom validation logic. The constraint is persisted to the database and will be applied to all subsequent create/update operations for objects of this type. Returns the created or updated constraint object. This operation affects validation behavior for the entire type.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate. Description lacks critical details about request body format, persistence, scope of effect, and return value. Added information about constraint definition structure, database persistence, application scope, and side effects to help AI agents understand the operation's impact.

message: Operation 'getPermissions' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary is clear. Description lacks context about what 'subject' means in Para's permission model, what permissions contain, and the use case. Added clarification that subjects can be users/roles/applications, explained permission structure and scope, and noted typical use cases for better AI agent interpretation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all permissions granted to a specific subject (user, role, or application). The subject is identified by its ID in the path parameter. Returns a permissions object containing the list of allowed actions and resources for the subject. Permissions determine what operations the subject can perform on Para objects and resources. Used to verify access control and authorization rules.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear. Description lacks context about what 'subject' means in Para's permission model, what permissions contain, and the use case. Added clarification that subjects can be users/roles/applications, explained permission structure and scope, and noted typical use cases for better AI agent interpretation.
